questionHtml:
Data is given for the reaction 2X2 (g) + Y2 (g) → 2X2Y (g).

What rate equation can be inferred from the data?

A.  Rate = k [X2] [Y2]

B.  Rate = k [X2]2 [Y2]

C.  Rate = k [X2]2 [Y2]0

D.  Rate = k [X2]2 [Y2]2

meta.title: Data is given for the reaction 2X2 (g) + Y2 (g) → 2X2Y (g). What rate equation can be inferred from the data? A. Rate = k [X2] [Y2] B. Rate = k [X2]2 [Y2] C. Rate = k [X2]2 [Y2]0 D
breadcrumbLabels: Reactivity 2. How much, how fast and how far?
Reactivity 2.2—How fast? The rate of chemical change
Reactivity 2.2.9—Rate equations depend on the mechanism of the reaction and can only be determined experimentally. Deduce the rate equation for a reaction from experimental data.
